req.title: Hr-Admin views timesheet
req.statement: An authenticated hr-admin shall be able to view a timesheet through the UI form. Mandatory fields must be validated and an audit record must be written on success.

tests.name: naive_case
tests.executable:
# Pytest Tests for HR Admin Timesheet View Requirement

```python
"""
Test Suite: HR Admin Timesheet View
Requirement: An authenticated hr-admin shall be able to view a timesheet
             through the UI form. Mandatory fields must be validated and
             an audit record must be written on success.
"""

import pytest
from unittest.mock import MagicMock, patch, call
from datetime import date, datetime
from http import HTTPStatus


# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
# Fixtures
# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------

@pytest.fixture
def hr_admin_user():
    """A fully authenticated HR-Admin user."""
    user = MagicMock()
    user.id = "usr-001"
    user.username = "jane.admin"
    user.role = "hr-admin"
    user.is_authenticated = True
    user.is_active = True
    return user


@pytest.fixture
def non_hr_admin_user():
    """An authenticated user who does NOT have the hr-admin role."""
    user = MagicMock()
    user.id = "usr-002"
    user.username = "john.employee"
    user.role = "employee"
    user.is_authenticated = True
    user.is_active = True
    return user


@pytest.fixture
def unauthenticated_user():
    """An unauthenticated (anonymous) user."""
    user = MagicMock()
    user.id = None
    user.username = None
    user.role = None
    user.is_authenticated = False
    user.is_active = False
    return user


@pytest.fixture
def valid_timesheet():
    """A complete, valid timesheet record."""
    return {
        "timesheet_id": "ts-1001",
        "employee_id": "emp-501",
        "employee_name": "Alice Smith",
        "period_start_date": date(2024, 1, 1),
        "period_end_date": date(2024, 1, 31),
        "total_hours": 160.0,
        "status": "submitted",
        "submitted_at": datetime(2024, 2, 1, 9, 0, 0),
        "line_items": [
            {
                "date": date(2024, 1, 2),
                "hours": 8.0,
                "project_code": "PROJ-100",
                "description": "Development work",
            }
        ],
    }


@pytest.fixture
def timesheet_missing_mandatory_fields():
    """A timesheet record where mandatory fields are absent/None."""
    return {
        "timesheet_id": "ts-1002",
        "employee_id": None,        # mandatory – missing
        "employee_name": "",        # mandatory – empty string
        "period_start_date": None,  # mandatory – missing
        "period_end_date": date(2024, 1, 31),
        "total_hours": None,        # mandatory – missing
        "status": "submitted",
        "submitted_at": datetime(2024, 2, 1, 9, 0, 0),
        "line_items": [],
    }


@pytest.fixture
def mock_timesheet_service(valid_timesheet):
    """Mock of the timesheet service layer."""
    service = MagicMock()
    service.get_timesheet_by_id.return_value = valid_timesheet
    return service


@pytest.fixture
def mock_audit_service():
    """Mock of the audit logging service."""
    return MagicMock()


@pytest.fixture
def mock_ui_form():
    """Mock of the UI form renderer/view."""
    form = MagicMock()
    form.render.return_value = {"status": "rendered", "fields": {}}
    return form


# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
# Helper / SUT (System-Under-Test) scaffolding
# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------

class TimesheetFormView:
    """
    Thin stand-in for the real application view so that tests are
    decoupled from a specific web framework.  Replace with the real
    import once the application exists.
    """

    MANDATORY_FIELDS = [
        "timesheet_id",
        "employee_id",
        "employee_name",
        "period_start_date",
        "period_end_date",
        "total_hours",
        "status",
    ]

    def __init__(self, timesheet_service, audit_service, ui_form):
        self.timesheet_service = timesheet_service
        self.audit_service = audit_service
        self.ui_form = ui_form

    def view(self, requesting_user, timesheet_id: str) -> dict:
        # 1. Authentication check
        if not requesting_user.is_authenticated:
            return {"status_code": HTTPStatus.UNAUTHORIZED,
                    "error": "Authentication required"}

        # 2. Authorisation check
        if requesting_user.role != "hr-admin":
            return {"status_code": HTTPStatus.FORBIDDEN,
                    "error": "Insufficient permissions"}

        # 3. Fetch timesheet
        timesheet = self.timesheet_service.get_timesheet_by_id(timesheet_id)
        if timesheet is None:
            return {"status_code": HTTPStatus.NOT_FOUND,
                    "error": "Timesheet not found"}

        # 4. Mandatory field validation
        validation_errors = self._validate_mandatory_fields(timesheet)
        if validation_errors:
            return {"status_code": HTTPStatus.UNPROCESSABLE_ENTITY,
                    "error": "Mandatory field validation failed",
                    "validation_errors": validation_errors}

        # 5. Render UI form
        rendered = self.ui_form.render(timesheet)

        # 6. Write audit record on success
        self.audit_service.write(
            actor=requesting_user.username,
            action="VIEW_TIMESHEET",
            resource_type="timesheet",
            resource_id=timesheet_id,
            timestamp=datetime.utcnow(),
        )

        return {"status_code": HTTPStatus.OK, "data": rendered}

    def _validate_mandatory_fields(self, timesheet: dict) -> list:
        errors = []
        for field in self.MANDATORY_FIELDS:
            value = timesheet.get(field)
            if value is None or value == "":
                errors.append(f"'{field}' is mandatory but was empty or missing")
        return errors


# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
# Test classes
# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------

class TestAuthentication:
    """Verify that only authenticated users can access the view endpoint."""

    def test_unauthenticated_user_receives_401(
        self, unauthenticated_user, mock_timesheet_service,
        mock_audit_service, mock_ui_form
    ):
        """An unauthenticated request must be rejected with 401."""
        sut = TimesheetFormView(mock_timesheet_service, mock_audit_service, mock_ui_form)

        response = sut.view(unauthenticated_user, "ts-1001")

        assert response["status_code"] == HTTPStatus.UNAUTHORIZED

    def test_unauthenticated_user_receives_error_message(
        self, unauthenticated_user, mock_timesheet_service,
        mock_audit_service, mock_ui_form
    ):
        """Error message should clearly indicate authentication is required."""
        sut = TimesheetFormView(mock_timesheet_service, mock_audit_service, mock_ui_form)

        response = sut.view(unauthenticated_user, "ts-1001")

        assert "Authentication required" in response["error"]

    def test_unauthenticated_user_does_not_trigger_audit(
        self, unauthenticated_user, mock_timesheet_service,
        mock_audit_service, mock_ui_form
    ):
        """No audit record should be written for an unauthenticated attempt."""
        sut = TimesheetFormView(mock_timesheet_service, mock_audit_service, mock_ui_form)

        sut.view(unauthenticated_user, "ts-1001")

        mock_audit_service.write.assert_not_called()

    def test_unauthenticated_user_does_not_fetch_timesheet(
        self, unauthenticated_user, mock_timesheet_service,
        mock_audit_service, mock_ui_form
    ):
        """The timesheet service must not be called for unauthenticated users."""
        sut = TimesheetFormView(mock_timesheet_service, mock_audit_service, mock_ui_form)

        sut.view(unauthenticated_user, "ts-1001")

        mock_timesheet_service.get_timesheet_by_id.assert_not_called()


class TestAuthorisation:
    """Verify that only hr-admin role users can view timesheets."""

    def test_non_hr_admin_receives_403(
        self, non_hr_admin_user, mock_timesheet_service,
        mock_audit_service, mock_ui_form
    ):
        """An authenticated non-hr-admin user must receive 403 Forbidden."""
        sut = TimesheetFormView(mock_timesheet_service, mock_audit_service, mock_ui_form)

        response = sut.view(non_hr_admin_user, "ts-1001")

        assert response["status_code"] == HTTPStatus.FORBIDDEN

    def test_non_hr_admin_receives_insufficient_permissions_message(
        self, non_hr_admin_user, mock_timesheet_service,
        mock_audit_service, mock_ui_form
    ):
        """Error message must indicate insufficient permissions."""
        sut = TimesheetFormView(mock_timesheet_service, mock_audit_service, mock_ui_form)

        response = sut.view(non_hr_admin_user, "ts-1001")

        assert "Insufficient permissions" in response["error"]

    def test_non_hr_admin_does_not_trigger_audit(
        self, non_hr_admin_user, mock_timesheet_service,
        mock_audit_service, mock_ui_form
    ):
        """No audit record should be created for an unauthorised access attempt."""
        sut = TimesheetFormView(mock_timesheet_service, mock_audit_service, mock_ui_form)

        sut.view(non_hr_admin_user, "ts-1001")

        mock_audit_service.write.assert_not_called()

    def test_non_hr_admin_does_not_fetch_timesheet(
        self, non_hr_admin_user, mock_timesheet_service,
        mock_audit_service, mock_ui_form
    ):
        """The timesheet service must not be queried for unauthorised users."""
        sut = TimesheetFormView(mock_timesheet_service, mock_audit_service, mock_ui_form)

        sut.view(non_hr_admin_user, "ts-1001")

        mock_timesheet_service.get_timesheet_by_id.assert_not_called()

    def test_hr_admin_is_permitted_access(
        self, hr_admin_user, mock_timesheet_service,
        mock_audit_service, mock_ui_form
    ):
        """An authenticated hr-admin must NOT receive a 401 or 403."""
        sut = TimesheetFormView(mock_timesheet_service, mock_audit_service, mock_ui_form)

        response = sut.view(hr_admin_user, "ts-1001")

        assert response["status_code"] not in (HTTPStatus.UNAUTHORIZED, HTTPStatus.FORBIDDEN)


class TestTimesheetRetrieval:
    """Verify correct behaviour when fetching a timesheet record."""

    def test_hr_admin_can_view_existing_timesheet(
        self, hr_admin_user, mock_timesheet_service,
        mock_audit_service, mock_ui_form
    ):
        """A valid request by hr-admin must return HTTP 200."""
        sut = TimesheetFormView(mock_timesheet_service, mock_audit_service, mock_ui_form)

        response = sut.view(hr_admin_user, "ts-1001")

        assert response["status_code"] == HTTPStatus.OK

    def test_correct_timesheet_id_passed_to_service(
        self, hr_admin_user, mock_timesheet_service,
        mock_audit_service, mock_ui_form
    ):
        """The service must be called with the exact timesheet_id requested."""
        sut = TimesheetFormView(mock_timesheet_service, mock_audit_service, mock_ui_form)

        sut.view(hr_admin_user, "ts-1001")

        mock_timesheet_service.get_timesheet_by_id.assert_called_once_with("ts-1001")

    def test_non_existent_timesheet_returns_404(
        self, hr_admin_user, mock_timesheet_service,
        mock_audit_service, mock_ui_form
    ):
        """Requesting a timesheet that does not exist must return 404."""
        mock_timesheet_service.get_timesheet_by_id.return_value = None
        sut = TimesheetFormView(mock_timesheet_service, mock_audit_service, mock_ui_form)

        response = sut.view(hr_admin_user, "ts-9999")

        assert response["status_code"] == HTTPStatus.NOT_FOUND

    def test_non_existent_timesheet_does_not_trigger_audit(
        self, hr_admin_user, mock_timesheet_service,
        mock_audit_service, mock_ui_form
    ):
        """No audit entry should be written when the timesheet is not found."""
        mock_timesheet_service.get_timesheet_by_id.return_value = None
        sut = TimesheetFormView(mock_timesheet_service, mock_audit_service, mock_ui_form)

        sut.view(hr_admin_user, "ts-9999")

        mock_audit_service.write.assert_not_called()

    def test_response_contains_rendered_form_data(
        self, hr_admin_user, mock_timesheet_service,
        mock_audit_service, mock_ui_form
    ):
        """The response payload must include the rendered UI form data."""
        sut = TimesheetFormView(mock_timesheet_service, mock_audit_service, mock_ui_form)

        response = sut.view(hr_admin_user, "ts-1001")

        assert "data" in response
        assert response["data"] is not None


class TestMandatoryFieldValidation:
    """Verify that mandatory fields on the timesheet form are validated."""

    @pytest.mark.parametrize("missing_field", [
        "timesheet_id",
        "employee_id",
        "employee_name",
        "period_start_date",
        "period_end_date",
        "total_hours",
        "status",
    ])
    def test_missing_mandatory_field_returns_unprocessable(
        self, hr_admin_user, mock_audit_service, mock_ui_form,
        valid_timesheet, missing_field
    ):
        """Each mandatory field individually absent must cause 422."""
        timesheet = dict(valid_timesheet)
        timesheet[missing_field] = None  # remove the mandatory value

        service = MagicMock()
        service.get_timesheet_by_id.return_value = timesheet
        sut = TimesheetFormView(service, mock_audit_service, mock_ui_form)

        response = sut.view(hr_admin_user, "ts-1001")

        assert response["status_code"] == HTTPStatus.UNPROCESSABLE_ENTITY

    @pytest.mark.parametrize("empty_string_field", [
        "timesheet_id",
        "employee_id",
        "employee_name",
        "status",
    ])
    def test_empty_string_mandatory_field_returns_unprocessable(
        self, hr_admin_user, mock_audit_service, mock_ui_form,
        valid_timesheet, empty_string_field
    ):
        """Mandatory string fields set to empty string must cause 422."""
        timesheet = dict(valid_timesheet)
        timesheet[empty_string_field] = ""

        service = MagicMock()
        service.get_timesheet_by_id.return_value = timesheet
        sut = TimesheetFormView(service, mock_audit_service, mock_ui_form)

        response = sut.view(hr_admin_user, "ts-1001")

        assert response["status_code"] == HTTPStatus.UNPROCESSABLE_ENTITY

    def test_validation_errors_listed_in_response(
        self, hr_admin_user, mock_audit_service, mock_ui_form,
        timesheet_missing_mandatory_fields
    ):
        """Response body must enumerate which fields failed validation."""
        service = MagicMock()
        service.get_timesheet_by_id.return_value = timesheet_missing_mandatory_fields
        sut = TimesheetFormView(service, mock_audit_service, mock_ui_form)

        response = sut.view(hr_admin_user, "ts-1002")

        assert "validation_errors" in response
        assert len(response["validation_errors"]) > 0

    def test_mandatory_field_failure_does_not_trigger_audit(
        self, hr_admin_user, mock_audit_service, mock_ui_form,
        timesheet_missing_mandatory_fields
    ):
        """No audit record must be written when mandatory field validation fails."""
        service = MagicMock()
        service.get_timesheet_by_id.return_value = timesheet_missing_mandatory_fields
        sut = TimesheetFormView(service, mock_audit_service, mock_ui_form)

        sut.view(hr_admin_user, "ts-1002")

        mock_audit_service.write.assert_not_called()

    def test_mandatory_field_failure_does_not_render_form(
        self, hr_admin_user, mock_audit_service, mock_ui_form,
        timesheet_missing_mandatory_fields
    ):
        """The UI form must not be rendered when validation fails."""
        service = MagicMock()
        service.get_timesheet_by_id.return_value = timesheet_missing_mandatory_fields
        sut = TimesheetFormView(service, mock_audit_service, mock_ui_form)

        sut.view(hr_admin_user, "ts-1002")

        mock_ui_form.render.assert_not_called()

    def test_all_mandatory_fields_present_passes_validation(
        self, hr_admin_user, mock_timesheet_service,
        mock_audit_service, mock_ui_form
    ):
        """A timesheet with all mandatory fields populated must pass validation."""
        sut = TimesheetFormView(mock_timesheet_service, mock_audit_service, mock_ui_form)

        response = sut.view(hr_admin_user, "ts-1001")

        assert response["status_code"] == HTTPStatus.OK
        assert "validation_errors" not in response


class TestAuditRecord:
    """Verify that an audit record is written on a successful timesheet view."""

    def test_audit_record_written_on_success(
        self, hr_admin_user, mock_timesheet_service,
        mock_audit_service, mock_ui_form
    ):
        """audit_service.write must be called exactly once on success."""
        sut = TimesheetFormView(mock_timesheet_service, mock_audit_service, mock_ui_form)

        sut.view(hr_admin_user, "ts-1001")

        mock_audit_service.write.assert_called_once()

    def test_audit_record_contains_actor(
        self, hr_admin_user, mock_timesheet_service,
        mock_audit_service, mock_ui_form
    ):
        """Audit entry must record the username of the actor."""
        sut = TimesheetFormView(mock_timesheet_service, mock_audit_service, mock_ui_form)

        sut.view(hr_admin_user, "ts-1001")

        _, kwargs = mock_audit_service.write.call_args
        assert kwargs["actor"] == hr_admin_user.username

    def test_audit_record_contains_action_view_timesheet(
        self, hr_admin_user, mock_timesheet_service,
        mock_audit_service, mock_ui_form
    ):
        """Audit entry must record the action as VIEW_TIMESHEET."""
        sut = TimesheetFormView(mock_timesheet_service, mock_audit_service, mock_ui_form)

        sut.view(hr_admin_user, "ts-1001")

        _, kwargs = mock_audit_service.write.call_args
        assert kwargs["action"] == "VIEW_TIMESHEET"

    def test_audit_record_contains_correct_resource_type(
        self, hr_admin_user, mock_timesheet_service,
        mock_audit_service, mock_ui_form
    ):
        """Audit entry must record the resource type as 'timesheet'."""
        sut = TimesheetFormView(mock_timesheet_service, mock_audit_service, mock_ui_form)

        sut.view(hr_admin_user, "ts-1001")

        _, kwargs = mock_audit_service.write.call_args
        assert kwargs["resource_type"] == "timesheet"

    def test_audit_record_contains_correct_resource_id(
        self, hr_admin_user, mock_timesheet_service,
        mock_audit_service, mock_ui_form
    ):
        """Audit entry must record the exact timesheet_id that was viewed."""
        sut = TimesheetFormView(mock_timesheet_service, mock_audit_service, mock_ui_form)

        sut.view(hr_admin_user, "ts-1001")

        _, kwargs = mock_audit_service.write.call_args
        assert kwargs["resource_id"] == "ts-1001"

    def test_audit_record_contains_timestamp(
        self, hr_admin_user, mock_timesheet_service,
        mock_audit_service, mock_ui_form
    ):
        """Audit entry must include a timestamp."""
        sut = TimesheetFormView(mock_timesheet_service, mock_audit_service, mock_ui_form)

        sut.view(hr_admin_user, "ts-1001")

        _, kwargs = mock_audit_service.write.call_args
        assert "timestamp" in kwargs
        assert isinstance(kwargs["timestamp"], datetime)

    def test_audit_not_written_more_than_once_per_view(
        self, hr_admin_user, mock_timesheet_service,
        mock_audit_service, mock_ui_form
    ):
        """Exactly one audit entry – not multiple – must be written per request."""
        sut = TimesheetFormView(mock_timesheet_service, mock_audit_service, mock_ui_form)

        sut.view(hr_admin_user, "ts-1001")

        assert mock_audit_service.write.call_count == 1


class TestUIFormRendering:
    """Verify correct UI form behaviour on a successful view request."""

    def test_ui_form_is_rendered_on_success(
        self, hr_admin_user, mock_timesheet_service,
        mock_audit_service, mock_ui_form
    ):
        """The UI form render method must be called exactly once on success."""
        sut = TimesheetFormView(mock_timesheet_service, mock_audit_service, mock_ui_form)

        sut.view(hr_admin_user, "ts-1001")

        mock_ui_form.render.assert_called_once()

    def test_ui_form_rendered_with_correct_timesheet_data(
        self, hr_admin_user, mock_timesheet_service,
        mock_audit_service, mock_ui_form, valid_timesheet
    ):
        """The UI form must be rendered using the fetched timesheet data."""
        sut = TimesheetFormView(mock_timesheet_service, mock_audit_service, mock_ui_form)

        sut.view(hr_admin_user, "ts-1001")

        mock_ui_form.render.assert_called_once_with(valid_timesheet)

    def test_response_data_matches_rendered_form_output(
        self, hr_admin_user, mock_timesheet_service,
        mock_audit_service, mock_ui_form
    ):
        """The response data payload must equal the output from ui_form.render."""
        expected_render = {"status": "rendered", "fields": {"employee_name": "Alice Smith"}}
        mock_ui_form.render.return_value = expected_render
        sut = TimesheetFormView(mock_timesheet_service, mock_audit_service, mock_ui_form)

        response = sut.view(hr_admin_user, "ts-1001")

        assert response["data"] == expected_render


class TestEndToEndHappyPath:
    """End-to-end happy-path scenario combining all steps."""

    def test_full_successful_view_flow(
        self, hr_admin_user, mock_timesheet_service,
        mock_audit_service, mock_ui_form, valid_timesheet
    ):
        """
        Given an authenticated hr-admin,
        When they request a valid timesheet with all mandatory fields,
        Then the form is rendered, HTTP 200 is returned, and an audit record is written.
        """
        sut = TimesheetFormView(mock_timesheet_service, mock_audit_service, mock_ui_form)

        response = sut.view(hr_admin_user, "ts-1001")

        # HTTP 200
        assert response["status_code"] == HTTPStatus.OK

        # Timesheet service called
        mock_timesheet_service.get_timesheet_by_id.assert_called_once_with("ts-1001")

        # Form rendered with timesheet data
        mock_ui_form.render.assert_called_once_with(valid_timesheet)

        # Audit written with correct fields
        mock_audit_service.write.assert_called_once()
        _, kwargs = mock_audit_service.write.call_args
        assert kwargs["actor"] == hr_admin_user.username
        assert kwargs["action"] == "VIEW_TIMESHEET"
        assert kwargs["resource_id"] == "ts-1001"
```

---

## Test Coverage Summary

| Category | Tests | What is verified |
|---|---|---|
| **Authentication** | 4 | Unauthenticated users get 401; no data leak; no audit written |
| **Authorisation** | 5 | Non-hr-admin gets 403; only `hr-admin` role is permitted |
| **Retrieval** | 5 | Correct timesheet fetched; 404 on missing record; no audit on 404 |
| **Mandatory Field Validation** | 6 | Each mandatory field enforced (parametrised); 422 returned; audit/render blocked on failure |
| **Audit Record** | 7 | Written once on success; contains actor, action, resource type, resource id, and timestamp |
| **UI Form Rendering** | 3 | Rendered once with correct data; response contains rendered output |
| **End-to-End Happy Path** | 1 | All steps fire in correct order with correct values |

### Key design decisions
- **Fixtures** isolate test data and dependencies — swap mock with a real service without changing tests.  
- **`@pytest.mark.parametrize`** covers every mandatory field with a single test definition, preventing gaps.  
- **`assert_called_once` / `assert_not_called`** enforce ordering and side-effect boundaries strictly.  
- The `TimesheetFormView` stub keeps tests framework-agnostic; replace with the real import once implemented.

# Pytest Tests for HR Admin Timesheet View Requirement

```python
"""
Test Suite: HR Admin Timesheet View
Requirement: An authenticated hr-admin shall be able to view a timesheet
             through the UI form. Mandatory fields must be validated and
             an audit record must be written on success.
"""

import pytest
from unittest.mock import MagicMock, patch, call
from datetime import date, datetime
from http import HTTPStatus


# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
# Fixtures
# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------

@pytest.fixture
def hr_admin_user():
    """A fully authenticated HR-Admin user."""
    user = MagicMock()
    user.id = "usr-001"
    user.username = "jane.admin"
    user.role = "hr-admin"
    user.is_authenticated = True
    user.is_active = True
    return user


@pytest.fixture
def non_hr_admin_user():
    """An authenticated user who does NOT have the hr-admin role."""
    user = MagicMock()
    user.id = "usr-002"
    user.username = "john.employee"
    user.role = "employee"
    user.is_authenticated = True
    user.is_active = True
    return user


@pytest.fixture
def unauthenticated_user():
    """An unauthenticated (anonymous) user."""
    user = MagicMock()
    user.id = None
    user.username = None
    user.role = None
    user.is_authenticated = False
    user.is_active = False
    return user


@pytest.fixture
def valid_timesheet():
    """A complete, valid timesheet record."""
    return {
        "timesheet_id": "ts-1001",
        "employee_id": "emp-501",
        "employee_name": "Alice Smith",
        "period_start_date": date(2024, 1, 1),
        "period_end_date": date(2024, 1, 31),
        "total_hours": 160.0,
        "status": "submitted",
        "submitted_at": datetime(2024, 2, 1, 9, 0, 0),
        "line_items": [

# Pytest Tests for HR Admin Timesheet View Requirement

```python
"""
Test Suite: HR Admin Timesheet View
Requirement: An authenticated hr-admin shall be able to view a timesheet
             through the UI form. Mandatory fields must be validated and
             an audit record must be written on success.
"""

import pytest
from unittest.mock import MagicMock, patch, call
from datetime import date, datetime
from http import HTTPStatus


# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
# Fixtures
# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------

@pytest.fixture
def hr_admin_user():
    """A fully authenticated HR-Admin user."""
    user = MagicMock()
    user.id = "usr-001"
    user.username = "jane.admin"
    user.role = "hr-admin"
    user.is_authenticated = True
    user.is_active = True
    return user


@pytest.fixture
def non_hr_admin_user():
    """An authenticated user who does NOT have the hr-admin role."""
    user = MagicMock()
    user.id = "usr-002"
    user.username = "john.employee"
    user.role = "employee"
    user.is_authenticated = True
    user.is_active = True
    return user


@pytest.fixture
def unauthenticated_user():
    """An unauthenticated (anonymous) user."""
    user = MagicMock()
    user.id = None
    user.username = None
    user.role = None
    user.is_authenticated = False
    user.is_active = False
    return user


@pytest.fixture
def valid_timesheet():
    """A complete, valid timesheet record."""
    return {
        "timesheet_id": "ts-1001",
        "employee_id": "emp-501",
        "employee_name": "Alice Smith",
        "period_start_date": date(2024, 1, 1),
        "period_end_date": date(2024, 1, 31),
        "total_hours": 160.0,
        "status": "submitted",
        "submitted_at": datetime(2024, 2, 1, 9, 0, 0),
        "line_items": [
            {
                "date": date(2024, 1, 2),
                "hours": 8.0,
                "project_code": "PROJ-100",
                "description": "Development work",
            }
        ],
    }


@pytest.fixture
def timesheet_missing_mandatory_fields():
    """A timesheet record where mandatory fields are absent/None."""
    return {
        "timesheet_id": "ts-1002",
        "employee_id": None,        # mandatory – missing
        "employee_name": "",        # mandatory – empty string
        "period_start_date": None,  # mandatory – missing
        "period_end_date": date(2024, 1, 31),
        "total_hours": None,        # mandatory – missing
        "status": "submitted",
        "submitted_at": datetime(2024, 2, 1, 9, 0, 0),
        "line_items": [],
    }


@pytest.fixture
def mock_timesheet_service(valid_timesheet):
    """Mock of the timesheet service layer."""
    service = MagicMock()
    service.get_timesheet_by_id.return_value = valid_timesheet
    return service


@pytest.fixture
def mock_audit_service():
    """Mock of the audit logging service."""
    return MagicMock()


@pytest.fixture
def mock_ui_form():
    """Mock of the UI form renderer/view."""
    form = MagicMock()
    form.render.return_value = {"status": "rendered", "fields": {}}
    return form


# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
# Helper / SUT (System-Under-Test) scaffolding
# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------

class TimesheetFormView:
    """
    Thin stand-in for the real application view so that tests are
    decoupled from a specific web framework.  Replace with the real
    import once the application exists.
    """

    MANDATORY_FIELDS = [
        "timesheet_id",
        "employee_id",
        "employee_name",
        "period_start_date",
        "period_end_date",
        "total_hours",
        "status",
    ]

    def __init__(self, timesheet_service, audit_service, ui_form):
        self.timesheet_service = timesheet_service
        self.audit_service = audit_service
        self.ui_form = ui_form
